markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire following the first vote to confirm Markwayne Mullin or their nomination being withdrawn.
markets.no_sub_title: Martin Heinrich
markets.rules_primary: If Martin Heinrich votes Yea for Markwayne Mullin for Secretary of Homeland Security before Jan 1, 2027, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
The market shall resolve to Yes if:

A vote results in the confirmation of the Nominee via a procedure that does not result in the recording of a tally of votes

The market shall resolve to No if:

Such a vote results in the rejection of the Nominee via a procedure that does not result in the recording of a tally of votes
Should a withdrawal of the nominee's nomination be formally communicated to the United States Senate
The nominee is withdrawn (or intent to nominate a nominee is withdrawn by the President or President-elect)

Should more than one Senate confirmation vote on the confirmation of the Nominee to the Office be held, the outcome of the first such vote will be used to resolve this market.

The outcomes of committee votes, hearings, or reports, cloture votes and other procedural votes will have no impact on the resolution of this market. Once the vote has been closed and the correct result of the vote has been announced, a request by Martin Heinrich to add, remove, or alter a vote shall not be considered for purposes of resolving this market, regardless of any change to the official vote tally reported by the website senate.gov or any other official source.
markets.title: Will Martin Heinrich vote for Markwayne Mullin?

markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if the vote occurs or if the nomination is withdrawn.
markets.no_sub_title: Above 54
markets.rules_primary: If the number of Senators voting Yea for the nomination of Markwayne Mullin to Secretary of Homeland Security after Issuance and before Jan 1, 2027 is above 54, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary: If no vote has occurred before Jan 1, 2027, then any market not including “zero” within its count (e.g. “Between 50 and 64”) resolves to No, and any market including “zero” (e.g. “Below 50”) resolves to Yes. If the vote count is not specified but the nominee is confirmed, any market including “100” (e.g. “Between 65 and 100”) will resolve to Yes, and any market not including “100” (e.g. “Below 65”) will resolve to No. Any vote cast by the Vice President on the nomination shall not be considered for purposes of this market. Once the market has expired, a request by any Senator to add, remove, or alter a vote shall not be considered for purposes of resolving this market, regardless of any change to the official vote tally reported by the website Senate.gov or any other official source.

markets.no_sub_title: Triple Double
markets.rules_primary: If the play by play or color commentator(s) says Triple Double as part of Orlando vs Cleveland Professional Basketball Game, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
Video of the Orlando vs Cleveland Professional Basketball Game will be primarily used to resolve the market; if a consensus by Kalshi employees cannot be reached using video, internal & external (official) transcripts of the Orlando vs Cleveland Professional Basketball Game will be used.

Payout Criterion: 
The exact phrase/word, or a plural or possessive form of the phrase/word, must be used. Grammatical/tense inflections are otherwise not included. 
Commentary will count once tip off has occurred and end once the game has concluded. Should the game go into overtime, the commentary will continue to count until the game has ended. If the final play is in review, commentary will end following the referee decision. For the purpose of this market, this market will resolve based on the national broadcast. If it is not nationally televised the home team local broadcast will be used.
Promotional content aired during the event will count, but advertisements will not. 
Except as otherwise specified, previously aired content, archival footage, and recordings captured at times, places, or events wholly separate from the featured address will not count.
Please see full rules for more details.

Event does not qualify:
If an event is definitively cancelled or the event fails to qualify under the Payout Criterion, or any other circumstance prevents normal resolution, then the market for "Event does not qualify" resolves to Yes and all other markets will resolve to No.  

Postponement:
If the event is postponed, and such a postponement is announced by the end of the following calendar day to be rescheduled to a time within 14 calendar days, the markets shall remain open.

If it is not nationally televised the home team local broadcast will be used.

Except as otherwise specified, previously aired content, archival footage, and recordings captured at times, places, or events wholly separate from the featured address will not count.

Event does not qualify:
If an event is definitively cancelled or the event fails to qualify under the Payout Criterion, or any other circumstance prevents normal resolution, then the market for "Event does not qualify" resolves to Yes and all other markets will resolve to No.

If the event is postponed, and such a postponement is announced by the end of the following calendar day to be rescheduled to a time within 14 calendar days, the markets shall remain open.

markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if certified election results are published.
markets.no_sub_title: Danish People's Party
markets.rules_primary: If Danish People's Party finishes in 3rd place in the 2026 Danish general election according to the certified results, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
Ranking is determined by the specified counting method:
For plurality voting: rank is based on vote count or percentage
For ranked choice voting: rank is determined by elimination order (runner-up is eliminated in final round for second place)
For two-round systems: rank is based on final round results if it proceeds to second round, otherwise first-round totals
For proportional representation: rank follows the electoral authority's final seat allocation order

In case of exact ties, markets resolve proportionally (1/number of tied entities). Write-in candidates achieving the specified rank resolve all named candidate markets to NO unless a specific "Write-in" or "Other" option exists. Candidates who withdraw or are disqualified after the filing deadline but remain on the ballot are ranked based on votes received. If the election is cancelled or postponed beyond expiration, all markets resolve to NO.
